mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 3.72% remains elevated relative to inflation (CPI at 326.030). A sustained FEDFUNDS level above 3.5% signals continued monetary restriction that keeps real rates positive, supporting higher 10-year Treasury yields through the expectation of persistent policy tightness over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with above-trend real rates
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines by more than 15 basis points over the 20-day window despite FEDFUNDS remaining above 3.5%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS at 735,904M) combined with housing starts (HOUST at 1,246K) indicate sustained consumer demand. Strong demand signals compress long-term real yields as markets price in persistent growth that justifies lower risk premiums, driving 10-year Treasury yields lower over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with sustained consumer activity
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 20 basis points despite both RSAFS and HOUST meeting trigger conditions, or the joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 4.4% combined with payroll employment at 159,526K indicates a tight labor market. Persistent labor tightness signals wage pressure and inflation persistence, which forces the Fed to maintain higher policy rates, supporting elevated 10-year Treasury yields over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: tight labor market with wage pressure expectations
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines by more than 15 basis points despite both UNRATE and PAYEMS meeting trigger conditions, or interaction signal produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO at 101.7935) at or above trend signals robust supply-side capacity. Strong production capacity reduces inflation expectations and allows the Fed to consider rate cuts, compressing 10-year Treasury yields downward over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: supply-driven disinflation with capacity slack emerging
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 10 basis points over the 5-day window despite INDPRO growth exceeding 0.3%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1 at 24,024.957B) growth trajectory combined with retail sales momentum indicates sustained aggregate demand. When demand growth outpaces supply growth, inflation expectations rise, forcing higher 10-year Treasury yields to compensate for inflation risk premium over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: above-trend growth with inflation acceleration risk
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines by more than 25 basis points over the 60-day window despite GDPC1 growth exceeding 2.5%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS at 3.72%) exceeds the inflation rate implied by CPI momentum, real rates are restrictive. A crossing condition where FEDFUNDS falls below 3.5% signals monetary easing that reduces real rates, compressing 10-year Treasury yields downward over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines by > 0.25pp in the preceding month
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with rate cuts beginning
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 15 basis points over the 20-day window following a FEDFUNDS decline of > 0.25pp, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate (currently 4.4%) signals labor market softening. When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, it indicates recession risk, which drives flight-to-quality demand for Treasuries, compressing 10-year yields downward over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in the preceding 3 months
regime_hint: labor market deterioration with recession risk emerging
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 15 basis points over the 20-day window despite UNRATE rising > 0.3pp, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST at 1,246K) and retail sales (RSAFS at 735,904M) jointly signal consumer confidence and housing demand. When both series decline simultaneously by more than 2% month-over-month, it signals demand destruction that reduces inflation expectations and allows 10-year Treasury yields to fall over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month change < -2% AND RSAFS month-over-month change < -2%
regime_hint: demand shock with synchronized contraction in housing and consumption
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 10 basis points over the 5-day window despite both HOUST and RSAFS declining > 2% month-over-month, or interaction signal produces Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
